summary: I reviewed the iteration-3 technical design (`design.md`) against the 18 business requirements (`requirements.md`) for coverage in both directions: every requirement addressed, and nothing designed that no requirement asks for. I traced each of R1–R18 to a concrete design component independently rather than relying on the design's own mapping table — surface placement and load-time truth (R1/R2/R15), the full R3–R13 plain-language state machine in `Pipelines::StatusSummary` (including the iteration-suppression, name-both, N-steps-collapse, failed-with-location, aborted→Canceled, and unconditional catch-all rules), live-push and ordering convergence via `Pipelines::BroadcastStatus` fired from the run transitions plus the essential `ManagerTick` seam (R14/R16), and the words-not-color / compact≡full accessibility guarantees (R17/R18). The only branch beyond the requirement list (Blocked/stuck) is a real reachable state explicitly justified under R12, and the previously over-scoped `as_of` timestamp was removed — so there is no under- or over-coverage. The iteration-3 edits were guide-alignment refinements that don't change coverage. I wrote a **pass** verdict with no findings to `verdict.json`.
